title: gastrochilus somae
intro: Gastrochilus somae, also known as the Somae's orchid or Somae's gale, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Gastrochilus somae include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil for topical use, and it can also be consumed as a decoction or added to other herbal remedies in traditional Indian medicine.
